label: k-means, mixture models & clustering

node.label: k-means, mixture models & clustering
node.definition: Clustering and mixture models divide observations into latent groups using distance, density, or probabilistic component assignments without task labels.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, k-means, mixture models & clustering is modeled as depending on Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Recommendation, ranking & personalization.
examples: atlas downstream link to Recommendation, ranking & personalization
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: k-means, mixture models & clustering follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
In this atlas graph, k-means, mixture models & clustering has a `represents_with` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations with `core` strength.

In this atlas graph, k-means, mixture models & clustering has an `optimizes_for` dependency on MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing because MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
